name: Chess with Different Armies
author: AGP
description: Chess with Different Armies (Ralph Betza, ~1996) — a Recognized Chess Variant. Ordinary 8×8 chess, except each side may field a different, equal-strength army of pieces. The Kings and Pawns are always the orthodox FIDE ones; the other six pieces come from one of four balanced armies: the Fabulous FIDEs (orthodox Rook/Knight/Bishop/Queen), the Colorbound Clobberers (Bede, Waffle, FAD, Cardinal — a Bishop-themed army of colourbound pieces), the Remarkable Rookies (Short Rook, Woody Rook, Half-Duck, Chancellor — an orthogonal army), and the Nutty Knights (Charging Rook, Fibnif, Charging Knight, Colonel — Knight-themed pieces that move differently forwards and backwards). Choose the White and Black armies (default FIDEs vs Clobberers). Castling, en passant, checkmate and the usual draws all apply; a promoted Pawn may become any piece from either army in play, and when a side's corner piece is colourbound its queenside castling uses Betza's special three-square king hop.
